与る: to participate in, to take part in, to play a part in
丹後: Tango (former province located in the north of present-day Kyoto Prefecture)
丹波: Tanba (former province located in parts of present-day Kyoto, Hyogo and Osaka prefectures)
云う: to say, to utter, to declare
今から: from now, from this moment, henceforth
仲よく: on good terms (with), on friendly terms (with), (getting along) well
伊勢: Ise (former province located in the major part of present-day Mie Prefecture and parts of Aichi and Gifu prefectures)
倍速: double speed (data transfer, video playback, etc.), 2X speed
倭寇: wokou, wakō, Japanese pirates who raided the Chinese and Korean coastlines between the 13th and 16th centuries
偶に: occasionally, once in a while, now and then
刑務: correctional matters (of a crime), prison affairs
勁草: wind-resistant blade of grass, resistant idea (metaphorically)
吾妻: eastern Japan (esp. Kamakura or Edo, from perspective of Kyoto or Nara), eastern provinces
呆然: dumbfounded, overcome with surprise, in blank amazement
和名: Japanese name (esp. for a plant or animal)
喰らう: to eat, to drink, to wolf
噛ます: to force someone to clamp with their teeth, to force into someone's mouth (e.g. a gag)
壁掛け: wall-mounted ornament, wall decoration, wall hanging
壱岐: Iki (former province located on Iki Island in present-day Nagasaki Prefecture)
夏ばて: suffering from summer heat, summer heat fatigue
尾張: Owari (former province located in the west of present-day Aichi Prefecture)
山本: foot of a mountain, base of a mountain
嵩む: to mount up, to pile up, to accumulate
帳票: financial records (account books, ledgers, vouchers, forms, etc.)
庄屋: village headman (esp. in the Kansai region)
度忘れ: lapse of memory, (something) slipping one's mind, forgetting for a moment something one knows well
建て: indicates storeys, structures, or materials used in a building
張り付ける: to paste, to stick, to affix
彦星: Altair (star in the constellation Aquila), Alpha Aquilae
律令: ritsuryō, criminal, administrative and civil codes of the Nara and Heian eras based on Chinese models
忘年: forgetting the hardships of the old year
惣領: eldest child, oldest child, first-born child
打っ: adds emphasis to the following verb or indicates that the action is done forcefully or violently
摘まむ: to pinch, to hold (between one's fingers), to pick up (with chopsticks, tweezers, etc.)
敵う: to match, to be a match for, to rival
昂ぶる: to become aroused (of emotions, nerves, etc.), to become excited, to become stirred up
昂る: to become aroused (of emotions, nerves, etc.), to become excited, to become stirred up
春田: empty rice field (between the harvest and spring)
暫し: for a short while, for a brief period, soon
木耳: cloud ear mushroom (Auricularia auricula-judae), wood ear mushroom, Jew's ear mushroom
梓弓: catalpa bow, spirit bow, bow used to summon spirits, made of Japanese cherry birch, catalpa, or other wood
椋鳥: grey starling (Sturnus cineraceus), gray starling, white-cheeked starling
椰子: palm tree (any tree of family Arecaceae)
檀家: family which supports a temple, parishioner
毬藻: marimo (variety of algae, Cladophora aegagropila), round green algae, Cladophora ball
没する: to sink, to go down, to set
泡盛: awamori, strong Okinawan liquor distilled from rice or millet
泣ける: to shed tears, to be moved to tears
泥濘む: to become muddy, to become slushy
浸ける: to soak (in), to steep, to dip
淡路: Awaji (former province located on Awaji Island in present-day Hyōgo Prefecture)
灯る: to be lit (e.g. candle, lamp, light bulb), to be lighted, to be burning
片づける: to put in order, to tidy up, to clean up
狼狽える: to be flustered, to lose one's presence of mind
現われる: to appear, to come in sight, to become visible
畏まる: to obey respectfully, to humble oneself, to sit straight (upright, respectfully, attentively)
皐月: fifth month of the lunar calendar
磨り潰す: to mash, to grind, to pulverize
稜線: ridgeline, lines of a mountain ridge
窓側: window-side, window (seat, e.g. on a plane)
粒餡: coarse anko, coarse sweet red bean paste
紀伊: Kii (former province located in present-day Wakayama and southern Mie prefectures)
紫蘇: shiso (Perilla frutescens var. crispa), perilla, beefsteak plant
綿飴: cotton candy, fairy floss, candy floss
編纂: compilation (esp. dictionary, history, law code), editing
縫いぐるみ: stuffed toy, stuffed animal, plush toy
置き: every ..., every other ..., at intervals of ...
肌荒れ: bad skin, rough skin, unhealthy skin
能登: Noto (former province located in the north of present-day Ishikawa Prefecture)
芋虫: hornworm (caterpillar of a hawk moth), (hairless) caterpillar
芙蓉: cotton rose (Hibiscus mutabilis), Confederate rose, dixie rosemallow
葉月: eighth month of the lunar calendar
蒲鉾: kamaboko, steamed seasoned fish paste, usu. in a semicylindrical shape on a strip of wood and sliced to go in soup, etc.
蕗の薹: edible flower bud of a giant butterbur plant
虐める: to ill-treat, to bully, to torment
蛍烏賊: firefly squid (Watasenia scintillans), sparkling enope squid
言える: to be possible to say, to be able to say
託する: to entrust (someone) with, to leave (a matter) with someone, to place under someone's care
豚汁: pork miso soup, miso soup with pork and vegetables
阪急: Hankyu (Kansai area railway and retail conglomerate)
際する: to arrive, to come to pass
際に: in case of, at that time, at this time
隼人: Hayato, ethnic group which lived in Kyūshū in ancient times and opposed the state of Yamato
頭から: from the beginning, without hesitation, flatly (e.g. refusing)
飽くまで: to the end, to the bitter end, to the last
駿河: Suruga (former province located in the central and northern parts of present-day Shizuoka Prefecture)
鯉幟: koinobori, carp streamer, carp-shaped windsock traditionally flown to celebrate the Children's Day in May
鯛焼き: fish-shaped pancake filled with bean jam
黎明: daybreak, dawn, gray of the morning (grey)
黎明期: dawn (of a new era, civilization, etc.), dawning
存ずる: to know, to be aware (of), to be acquainted (with)
未だに: still, even now, to this day
表わす: to represent, to signify, to stand for
話せる: to be able to speak, to be able to talk
読める: to be able to read, to be legible, to be decipherable
連れて: bringing along, accompanied by, brought along
